rationale.R1.2: A general review of the section centrally involves checking whether the reasoning from curvature feedback to optimization benefit is coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes empirical claims about benchmark performance and overhead, so review should assess whether those claims are aligned with the reported evidence.
rationale.R1.6: Because the delegation asks to review a prose section, sentence-level clarity, hedging, flow, and concision are central responsibilities.
rationale.RX.1: The reviewer should preserve uncertainty where the artifact presents preliminary or tentative claims.
rationale.RX.2: The review should identify and avoid strengthening claims beyond the limited empirical support described.
rationale.RX.3: The task is only to review the section, so responsibilities should remain confined to section-level research-writing assessment.
rationale.RX.4: Because optimization claims can influence downstream methodological choices, the review should guard against unsupported performance implications.
rationale.RX.5: Any review judgment should remain traceable to statements in the provided section rather than unstated assumptions.
